verses.text: Woe to Moab: Nebo is spoiled, her cities cry out, the little ones make a cry of destruction. The proud land is broken. No nation's pride can stand when God arises to judge.
Woe to Moab: Nebo is spoiled, her cities cry out, the little ones make a cry of destruction. The proud land is broken. No nation's pride can stand when God arises to judge.
Woe to Moab: Nebo is spoiled, her cities cry out, the little ones make a cry of destruction. The proud land is broken. No nation's pride can stand when God arises to judge.
Woe to Moab: Nebo is spoiled, her cities cry out, the little ones make a cry of destruction. The proud land is broken. No nation's pride can stand when God arises to judge.
Weeping fills the way of descent; the spoiler comes on every city, and none escapes. Trust in works and treasures fails. When God's judgment comes, no defence avails.
Weeping fills the way of descent; the spoiler comes on every city, and none escapes. Trust in works and treasures fails. When God's judgment comes, no defence avails.

Moab was 'at ease from his youth,' settled on his lees, never emptied from vessel to vessel; so his taste remained. Untroubled prosperity bred complacency. Ease without affliction can leave a soul unrefined and unready.

How say they, 'We are mighty'? Moab is spoiled, the strong staff broken. Bemoan him, all his neighbours. Boasted strength is shattered. Human might is a fragile thing in the hand of God.

Come down from your glory and sit in thirst, O daughter of Dibon; Moab is confounded. Declare it abroad. Glory turns to shame. The exalted are brought low when they forget their Maker.
Come down from your glory and sit in thirst, O daughter of Dibon; Moab is confounded. Declare it abroad. Glory turns to shame. The exalted are brought low when they forget their Maker.
Come down from your glory and sit in thirst, O daughter of Dibon; Moab is confounded. Declare it abroad. Glory turns to shame. The exalted are brought low when they forget their Maker.
Judgment falls on city after city; 'the horn of Moab is cut off, and his arm is broken.' His power and pride are gone. God strips the proud of all they trusted in.
Judgment falls on city after city; 'the horn of Moab is cut off, and his arm is broken.' His power and pride are gone. God strips the proud of all they trusted in.

Make Moab drunken, for he magnified himself against the LORD; let her people leave the cities and dwell in the rock. Pride is repaid. Those who exalt themselves against God are made to flee for their lives.

Make Moab drunken, for he magnified himself against the LORD; let her people leave the cities and dwell in the rock. Pride is repaid. Those who exalt themselves against God are made to flee for their lives.

Make Moab drunken, for he magnified himself against the LORD; let her people leave the cities and dwell in the rock. Pride is repaid. Those who exalt themselves against God are made to flee for their lives.
'We have heard the pride of Moab... his loftiness, and his arrogancy'; his lies shall not avail. God has marked his haughtiness. The boasting He overhears He will surely answer.
'We have heard the pride of Moab... his loftiness, and his arrogancy'; his lies shall not avail. God has marked his haughtiness. The boasting He overhears He will surely answer.
Therefore He howls for Moab; the vine of Sibmah is wasted; joy and gladness are taken from the field. Even the Judge laments. God takes no pleasure in the ruin His justice requires.
Therefore He howls for Moab; the vine of Sibmah is wasted; joy and gladness are taken from the field. Even the Judge laments. God takes no pleasure in the ruin His justice requires.
Therefore He howls for Moab; the vine of Sibmah is wasted; joy and gladness are taken from the field. Even the Judge laments. God takes no pleasure in the ruin His justice requires.
From Heshbon to Elealeh rises the cry; He makes their offerings cease, and every head is bald with grief. Their religion and their mirth alike are stripped. Idolatry ends in mourning, not in plenty.
From Heshbon to Elealeh rises the cry; He makes their offerings cease, and every head is bald with grief. Their religion and their mirth alike are stripped. Idolatry ends in mourning, not in plenty.
From Heshbon to Elealeh rises the cry; He makes their offerings cease, and every head is bald with grief. Their religion and their mirth alike are stripped. Idolatry ends in mourning, not in plenty.
From Heshbon to Elealeh rises the cry; He makes their offerings cease, and every head is bald with grief. Their religion and their mirth alike are stripped. Idolatry ends in mourning, not in plenty.
On every housetop is lamentation, for Moab is broken like a vessel none desires. Shame covers her. What men prize, God can shatter and make worthless.
On every housetop is lamentation, for Moab is broken like a vessel none desires. Shame covers her. What men prize, God can shatter and make worthless.

The foe shall swoop like an eagle and spread his wings over Moab, who shall be destroyed for magnifying himself against the LORD. Pride is the root. To exalt oneself against God is to invite destruction.

The foe shall swoop like an eagle and spread his wings over Moab, who shall be destroyed for magnifying himself against the LORD. Pride is the root. To exalt oneself against God is to invite destruction.

The foe shall swoop like an eagle and spread his wings over Moab, who shall be destroyed for magnifying himself against the LORD. Pride is the root. To exalt oneself against God is to invite destruction.
'Fear, and the pit, and the snare' are upon Moab; he that flees the one falls into the other. There is no escape. The judgments of God hem the sinner in on every side.
'Fear, and the pit, and the snare' are upon Moab; he that flees the one falls into the other. There is no escape. The judgments of God hem the sinner in on every side.

The fugitives stand spent under Heshbon's shadow; woe to Moab; yet 'I will bring again the captivity of Moab in the latter days.' Mercy follows judgment. Even on a proud nation God's grace leaves a door of hope.
